🌅 Market News Digest
[Jun 24–25, 2026 EST]

🔥 Top Stories
• Venezuela quake turns catastrophic — USGS says the 7.1+ quake may be the strongest since 1900; death toll now 164 with rescue aid mobilizing.
• Hormuz tension eases, oil slides — Oman said no transit tolls, Maersk resumed crossings, and Brent/WTI erased war gains on stronger Gulf supply.
• AI chips rally tech — Micron’s blowout outlook sparked a Nasdaq futures jump; SK Hynix surged on U.S. listing chatter and chip demand optimism.
• BOJ Tamura signals more hikes — says Japan has effectively hit 2% inflation and rates should move toward neutral, supporting yen/bond volatility.

⛽ Oil & Energy
• Trump presses Big Oil — called Chevron, Exxon and BP over gasoline prices; Chevron said output growth is running 7%–10%.
• Middle East flows rise — Saudi prepares to restart Ras Tanura exports; Iraq says it is not quitting OPEC, but wants higher quotas.
• Oil prices weaken — Brent fell to about $72.74 and WTI to the lowest since Feb. 27 as supply fears eased.

📊 Markets & Macro
• Asia rallies on tech and rates — Nikkei +4% to +5%, Kospi +5%+, helped by Micron, while Australia lagged after solid jobs data.
• Australia jobs beat — May employment +40.3k, unemployment held at 4.4%, reducing urgency for RBA cuts.
• Japan bonds/yields swing — long JGB yields eased as the market digested Tamura’s hawkish comments and stronger equities.
• US futures firmer — Nasdaq 100 futures +2% and S&P 500 futures +0.6% as tech sentiment rebounded.

🌍 Geopolitical
• Hormuz corridor remains flashpoint — Iran’s IRGC warned against unilateral shipping routes; US/UAE/Oman said no toll regime, ships continued through the strait.
• Israel-Lebanon de-escalation — Israel pulled back from part of its southern Lebanon buffer zone in a goodwill step, per US officials.
• Russia-Ukraine strikes persist — Ukraine hit oil/refinery targets in Russia; Russia said it downed 269 drones overnight.

🪙 Crypto & DeFi
• Stablecoin and exchange headlines — Polymarket’s PUSD topped $500M supply fast; Ripple’s $RLUSD was approved for use in Japan.
• Security/regulatory pressure — WSJ said $3.84B moved through CoinEx tied to Iran sanctions evasion; Bithumb was fined for user data transfer.
• Bitcoin under pressure — miners face margin squeeze and a large options expiry risks adding to downside.

🏛️ Regulatory & Policy
• US-EU trade deal cleared — EU gave final approval just before Trump’s deadline.
• Banks pass stress tests — major U.S. lenders cleared the Fed exam, keeping dividend/buyback upside alive.
• ECB hawkish tone — Schnabel said rates may still need to rise further to return inflation to target.
• UK steel protection tightened — tariff-free steel quotas cut 51% from July 1.

🏢 Corporate
• Qualcomm ups long-term targets — now sees $40B non-handset revenue by FY2029, led by AI data centers and auto.
• Morgan Stanley, Citi, Wells Fargo boost payouts — MS raised dividend and reauthorized $20B buybacks; Citi/WFC also lifted dividends.
• Merck to buy Bio-Techne — agreed to pay $73/share in cash.
• Amazon doubles down on AI — plans an extra $13B investment in India and keeps hiring grads despite AI adoption.
